smartlead_campaign: OR #6 | Personalized Line | Forecast Lead Magnet | Without Other Contact Name

thread.subject: Braids and Wigs ad report
thread.body_text:
Maria, saw braids and wigs leans on same day shipping plus local delivery in about an hour, curious how that's been landing.

 separate note: I made Braids and Wigs a 4-min ad fatigue report from your live Meta ads. the one about to die, the duplicate eating budget, which to scale, and a fresh angle to test.

 most ecom brands in your niche usually run the same handful of ads too long. this spots it before ROAS drops.

 worth a look?

 Kyle H.
 Co-founder | OmniRocket

 P.S. if there's a better person for this, point me their way :)

thread.subject: RE: Braids and Wigs ad report
thread.body_text:
Maria - forgot to mention: the report also catches the ad Meta is quietly throttling. it's usually a near-copy of another ad that's eating budget without you noticing.

 Braids and Wigs's is still sitting ready on my end - want me to send it?

 - Kyle

 P.S. if it's not you, who should I reach out to?

thread.subject: RE: Braids and Wigs ad report
thread.body_text:
Maria - last check on this.

 should I shelve Braids and Wigs's fatigue report, or is there a better week to send it? no worries either way - just don't want to keep landing in your inbox.

 - Kyle

 P.S. if timing's just off, ping me in a few weeks - still happy to send.

Hi Kyle,
 

 Thank you for following up. Before we move forward, could you clarify what you'd need from us, specifically, did you need access to our Meta ad account to put this report together and if so, what level?
 

 Best regards,
 

 
 Maria Mushi 
 Manager – Braids and Wigs 
 London, Ontario 
 226-504-1321 
 www.braidsandwigs.ca

Hi Maria,

 great question, and the short answer is nothing needed on your end for what i already built.

here's the report btw: Braids and Wigs Ad Fatigue Report 

 this report was built entirely from public data. i pulled your live ad from meta's ad library and analyzed them from the outside, so no account access was involved at all.

 access only comes into play for the deeper work: a full audit plus a 30 and 90 day plan mapped to your actual numbers.

 the way we work is we hop on a quick call first, i get to understand your brand and where you're trying to go, and if we're a good fit we move forward. that's the point where i'd need access, and we'd start with read-only.

 worth a 30 min chat to see if it's a fit?

 Book a time here → Calendar 

 - Kyle

copy.tldr: Braids and Wigs is a value-driven Canadian hair retailer running only one weak 'Send message' ad, so the whole opening is that they have almost no real paid ads and a low average order size we need to lift with bundles.

deep_dive.what_they_are: A Canadian online beauty store based in London, Ontario, selling wigs, braiding hair, extensions, and hair and skin care from name brands like Sensationnel, Mane Concept, Eco Style, and Cantu.
deep_dive.what_they_sell: About 500 products. A wide range from tiny accessories under a dollar up to human hair wigs over $500. Their median price is about $10.52, which we are using as a stand-in for average order value since we cannot see their real numbers.
deep_dive.positioning: High-variety value play. Big catalog of known brands at low prices, with pay-later options and fast local delivery in London Ontario. They are not a premium boutique. They compete on price and selection.
deep_dive.who_buys: Value-conscious Canadian women who wear protective styles - wigs, braids, weaves, crochet - and want name-brand looks fast, affordably, and with easy pay-later.
strengths: Strong offers already in place: free shipping over $69.99, a free gift at $99.99, buy now pay later, and same day local delivery in London.
Real name brands people already search for by name, like Sensationnel and Mane Concept, plus a deep catalog that supports repeat buying.
gaps: Only one live Meta ad and it is a weak 'Send message' ad, not a shop or purchase ad. They are barely advertising at all.
No reviews or social proof anywhere on the site, which makes higher-priced wigs harder to sell cold.

game_plan.headline: Turn a single weak message ad into a proper testing setup that finds which hair style and which shopper actually buys, while lifting order size past the free shipping line.

phases.goal: Find out which product angle and which audience brings in buyers at a profit. We are learning, not scaling yet.
campaigns: One sales campaign built to drive purchases on the site, not messages. Three ad sets, each aimed at one of the three avatars below.
One retargeting ad set for people who viewed products or added to cart but did not buy, leaning on the free gift at $99.99 and pay-later.
lead_products: Human Hair Water Wave Headband Wig ($145.10) - a glueless, easy-install wig. Headband wigs are beginner friendly and photograph well, so this is a strong hook for the quick-and-easy shopper.
Human Hair Kinky Headband Wig ($123.34) - same easy-install story, different texture, good for split testing which look pulls more.
French Curl Braiding Hair 28 inches ($5.80) - very low price, high volume item that pulls in the braider audience cheaply and gets carts started toward the $69.99 free shipping line.
Mane Concept Brown Sugar Wig ($65.29, was $101.57) - a clear on-sale deal that fits the deal-driven under-$100 shopper.
phases.creative: Simple, real-looking video and photo of the install and the final look. Lead with the easy glueless install and the price. Show the offers plainly: free shipping over $69.99, free press-on nails at $99.99, and pay-later. Test three angles - 'ready to wear in minutes' for the headband wigs, 'name brands for less' for the on-sale wigs, and 'stock up for your next braid style' for the braiding hair. Change the ad goal from Send message to Shop or purchase.
phases.budget: Start around $50 to $70 CAD a day across the three ad sets, so each angle gets a fair test. Hold this steady for the full 30 days before judging.
phases.what_success_looks_like: Clear signal on which of the three angles and audiences brings buyers cheapest, a working purchase-focused campaign replacing the message ad, and average order value creeping toward the $69.99 free shipping line through bundles.

estimates.label: Starting budget
estimates.basis: Enough to get roughly 30 to 40 clicks a day at typical costs in this category, spread across three angles, which is the floor for learning what works in 30 days.

estimates.label: Average order value we are planning around
estimates.value: About $10.52 to start, aiming to lift it past $69.99
estimates.basis: $10.52 is the median price across their 500 products, used as a stand-in since we cannot see real order data. The plan pushes bundles to clear the $69.99 free shipping line.

estimates.label: Revenue we would aim to add
estimates.value: We would set this after the call, once we know real order size and margin
estimates.basis: We have no access to their sales, spend, or return numbers, so any revenue figure now would be a guess. We need their true average order value and margin first.

assumptions: Their real average order value is higher than $10.52 because wigs run $65 to $145. If most orders are tiny accessories, paid ads get much harder and the plan leans harder on bundles.
The one 'Send message' ad is close to all they are running. If they have a bigger account we cannot see, the plan changes.
Their margin on wigs is healthy enough to pay for ads. We must confirm this on the call.
Checkout, shipping, and pay-later all work smoothly. We have not tested a real purchase.

questions.question: What are you spending on Meta ads right now, and roughly what does a typical order come out to?
questions.listening_for: Whether they spend anything at all, and if their real order size is close to $10.52 or much higher because of wig sales. Low order size plus no budget makes this hard.

questions.question: Who runs your ads today - you, someone in-house, or another agency?
questions.listening_for: If Maria does it herself part time, if there is a past agency, or nobody. Tells us how much room there is and what we are replacing.

questions.question: Last time you ran ads to sell products, not just messages, what happened?
questions.listening_for: Whether they have ever run a real sales ad, what broke, and what they think worked. If they have never tried, expectations need setting.

questions.question: On a wig like the water wave headband at $145, roughly how much of that is profit after the product cost?
questions.listening_for: Whether margin can carry paid ads. If margin is thin on brand-name wigs, we need volume or higher-margin items to lead with.

questions.question: When someone messages you from that ad, do they usually end up buying, or does it mostly go quiet?
questions.listening_for: Whether the Send message ad actually converts. If it does, we keep a version of it. If it leaks, we know why sales are low.

questions.question: What is your best seller, and is it a wig, braiding hair, or a hair care product?
questions.listening_for: Confirms or corrects our guess to lead with the headband wigs. Their real best seller should be the lead product.

questions.question: Do most orders clear the $69.99 free shipping line, or come in under it?
questions.listening_for: Tells us how big the order-size problem is. If most carts are small, lifting order value is the main job.

questions.question: Why are there no customer reviews on the site - do you collect them anywhere, like Instagram or texts?
questions.listening_for: Whether social proof exists somewhere we can pull into ads. No proof at all makes the higher-priced wigs harder to sell cold.

questions.question: How much of your business is local London delivery versus shipping across Canada?
questions.listening_for: Whether to split budget by geography. Strong local pull means a cheap local delivery angle we can lean on hard.

questions.question: Do people ever find you by searching brand names like Sensationnel or Mane Concept, or 'braiding hair London Ontario'?
questions.listening_for: Real search demand. People search these brands and 'braiding hair near me' by name, so Google Search ads are an honest fit later.

questions.question: When someone in London wants braiding hair today, do they find you on Google, or go to a local shop?
questions.listening_for: Whether local search and a Google Business listing could capture same-day intent that Meta cannot.

objections.objection: We are barely spending on ads now, so this feels like a big jump.
objections.answer: That is exactly why we start small - around $50 to $70 a day for 30 days just to learn. We are not asking you to scale spend before we know what sells. We find the winner first, then grow it.

objections.objection: Our prices are low, how can ads pay for themselves?
objections.answer: Low prices are why we build carts past your own $69.99 free shipping line and lead with your $65 to $145 wigs, not the $1 accessories. We would confirm your real order size and margin on the call before promising anything.

objections.objection: The message ad brings us chats, why change it?
objections.answer: Chats are fine, but they need someone to reply and close. A purchase ad lets people buy at 2am without waiting on you. We can keep a message version running too and just add a real sales campaign beside it.

objections.objection: Do you need access to my ad account or my Shopify?
objections.answer: Everything so far was built from public data with no access. For the deeper work we would start with read-only access, nothing that lets us spend or change your store. You stay in full control.

red_flags.flag: Median product price is only about $10.52 and much of the catalog is cheap accessories under a few dollars.
red_flags.why_it_matters: If real orders are that small, paid ads cannot pay for themselves. The whole plan depends on leading with wigs and building bigger carts, so we must confirm real order size early.

red_flags.flag: No reviews or social proof anywhere on the site.
red_flags.why_it_matters: Selling a $145 wig to a cold stranger is hard with zero proof. We need to find reviews on Instagram or texts, or expect weaker results until proof exists.

red_flags.flag: The only live ad uses a Send message goal, not a purchase goal.
red_flags.why_it_matters: This suggests they may not be set up to track or drive online sales properly. Pixel, catalog, and checkout tracking all need checking before we spend.

red_flags.flag: Prices show in USD on a Canada-focused store with a huge currency switcher.
red_flags.why_it_matters: Mixed or confusing currency at checkout can scare off Canadian buyers and hurt conversion. Worth confirming what shoppers actually see at checkout.

other_channel_openings: Google Search ads on brand names people already type, like Sensationnel and Mane Concept, plus terms like 'braiding hair Canada'. This is real search demand, not a new category.
Local search and a Google Business listing for 'braiding hair London Ontario' and 'wigs near me', which pairs with their same-day local delivery.
